reasoning: The April 2025 IMF WEO officially projects India to overtake Japan as the world's 4th largest economy in 2025. However, the projected margin is incredibly thin: India at $4,187.017 billion versus Japan at $4,186.431 billion. This difference of just ~$0.58 billion (about 0.014%) is basically a rounding error. As such, the determination of which economy will be larger in the October 2025 WEO (which dictates the end-of-2025 resolution) will depend entirely on slight GDP data revisions and, critically, exchange rate volatility between now and September 2025. While India has a much higher structural real and nominal growth rate providing upside momentum, the Japanese Yen is significantly undervalued historically and any slight strengthening (or further Indian Rupee depreciation) could easily flip the ranking back to Japan. Given this essentially tied projection, the situation represents a near coin-toss, so a probability just over 50% is warranted to respect India's underlying faster nominal GDP growth while acknowledging the massive exchange rate uncertainty.
